sourceMovie.title: Endings, Beginnings
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Endings, Beginnings (2020) stuck because it commits to its romance premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Drake Doremus's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The romance and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Illusionist
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Endings, Beginnings clicked for you, The Illusionist (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Endings, Beginnings?
faq.answer: Try The Illusionist, The Dreamers, Café Society—each shares DNA with Endings, Beginnings while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Endings, Beginnings?
faq.answer: Endings, Beginnings (2020) is best described as Romance, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Endings, Beginnings worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
